senses.definition: ‌a weakness in someone's character or argument that can be used against them

examples.x: John's tendency to gossip is a chink in his armor - his otherwise professional reputation is marred by his inability to keep a secret.
The company's security system had a chink in its armor - a vulnerability that hackers were able to exploit.

senses.definition: in a state of disadvantage, difficulty, or vulnerability

examples.x: His opponents believe he is definitely on the run.
The team was on the run from a strong opponent, struggling to keep up with the pace and intensity of the game.
You've got 'em on the run now, boys!

senses.definition: a point of weakness or vulnerability
to do something that will lead to one's own failure

examples.x: He has been digging his own grave with a string of poor results.
The coach dug his own grave when he publicly insulted the team's owner.

senses.definition: to do something that causes one serious trouble or harm

examples.x: By lowering prices so much, the company has essentially cut its own throat by making a profit all but impossible.
By posting those private messages publicly, she cut her own throat and destroyed any chance of a political career.
The employees' threats to strike cut their own throats because management simply closed the facility and moved operations overseas.

senses.definition: used to convey that a person is causing harm to themselves through their own actions, choices, or behavior

examples.x: John's tendency to overthink things often makes him his own worst enemy, preventing him from taking action and moving forward.
Sarah's self-doubt and lack of confidence is often her own worst enemy, holding her back from success.
The company's short-term focus and reluctance to invest for the future makes it its own worst enemy, hampering its long-term growth.
Ignoring your health can make you your worst enemy.
Negative self-talk can easily turn you into your worst enemy.

senses.definition: to behave in a way that damages or undermines one's own reputation or success

examples.x: By treating his employees poorly, the manager has fouled his own nest by creating an unhappy and unproductive workplace.
The company fouled its own nest by releasing toxic chemicals into the local environment, alienating the very community it depended on for workers and customers.
The country's excessive military spending has fouled its own nest by bankrupting the economy and leaving little funds for infrastructure and social programs.

word_list.word: [kill] the goose that lays the golden egg
word_list.pronunciation: kˈɪl ðə ɡˈuːs ðæt lˈeɪz ðə ɡˈoʊldən ˈɛɡ

senses.definition: to destroy something that could bring one a lot of money or great success, particularly because of greed

examples.x: By pushing his employees too hard, the manager killed the goose that lays the golden egg by causing high turnover and losing his most productive staff.
By raising prices too high, the company risked killing the goose that lays the golden egg by alienating its customer base and threatening its profits.
When the mining company overextracted resources from the land, it killed the goose that lays the golden egg by damaging the environment that provided the natural wealth in the first place.

word_list.word: the final nail in the coffin

senses.definition: the last event, action, or circumstance that causes something to fail or end

examples.x: Rising interest rates were the final nail in the coffin for many overextended homeowners, pushing them into default and foreclosure.
The data breach was the final nail in the coffin for the company's already damaged reputation - customers lost all trust after that.
When the star witness changed her story, it was the final nail in the coffin for the defendant's case.

senses.definition: (of a difficult or challenging situation) to overwhelm someone and cause them stress, anxiety, or other negative emotions

examples.x: The amount of work we have to do this week is getting on top of me.
The constant demands and deadlines at work are really getting on top of her.
The pressure of the upcoming exams is really getting on top of me.

senses.definition: to become satisfied with one's past achievements and to stop making efforts to improve or progress
in an uncertain or unstable situation that is likely to fail or collapse

examples.x: The athlete's performance in the last game was on shaky ground, and some are questioning whether they will continue to be an asset to the team.
The company's financial situation is on shaky ground, and they may not be able to stay afloat much longer.
The politician's position on the issue is on shaky ground, and they may suffer electoral consequences if they do not clarify their stance.
The relationship between the two countries is on shaky ground, and there are concerns about rising tensions between them.
The scientist's theory is on shaky ground, and further research is needed to confirm or disprove their findings.

senses.definition: to stop or put an end to something abruptly or decisively

examples.x: The company decided to knock the project on the head because it was too expensive to continue and was not generating enough revenue.

senses.definition: to lose momentum, energy, or enthusiasm for something, especially a task or project

examples.x: The company's marketing campaign started out strong, but it has been losing steam in recent weeks as interest from consumers has waned.
The writer started the book with a lot of momentum, but they have been losing steam as they struggle to develop the plot and characters.

senses.definition: an event or action that results in the failure of something, particularly an organization

examples.x: Being caught in a scandal can be the kiss of death for a public figure's reputation.
Hiring a bad manager can be the kiss of death for a company's success.
Losing the support of a key ally can be the kiss of death for a political campaign.
Receiving a negative review from a top critic can be the kiss of death for a new movie.

senses.definition: to experience a significant loss, failure, or setback in a particular situation

examples.x: The athlete took a beating in the championship game, losing by a wide margin.
The company's stock has taken a beating in the market due to poor financial performance.
The politician's approval rating has taken a beating in recent weeks due to a scandal.
